residualRisks: The new parchment and embedded frame treatments rely on token-driven mixes and should still be spot-checked in any external host surfaces beyond the repo stories and mock previews.
implementation.summary: Expanded the shared CodeBlock, CodeEditor, and DiffViewer APIs with reusable frame, density, file metadata, and asymmetric diff layout controls; updated the direct stories/tests to cover metadata-heavy, embedded, alternate-frame, empty, and one-sided states; and replaced the recurring Seraph/chat wrapper patterns with the shared surface APIs.

instructions: Run each required command in the repository and capture whether it passed.
Required commands are part of the acceptance criteria: lint, tests, package build, and Storybook build.
If a command fails, include the failing command, the important error summary, and whether the failure blocks acceptance.
Do not skip a command because a previous one failed; run the full sweep unless the repo becomes unusable.
prompt.outputFormat:
JSON with:
  checks: Array<{ command: string, passed: boolean, summary: string }>,
  allPassed: boolean,
  blockers: string[]
prompt.role: frontend quality engineer validating a reusable component family
prompt.task: Run the required local validation gates for the code-surface expansion and report the results.

description: Run the required local validation commands and report pass/fail details without using shell-kind subtasks.
title: Validate lint, test, build, and Storybook gates

residualRisks: The new parchment and embedded frame treatments rely on token-driven mixes and should still be spot-checked in any external host surfaces beyond the repo stories and mock previews.
